paragraphs.text: THE ANCHORITE JOHN THE HUMBLE H Abba Thomas. He came into Theoupolis to attend to the needs of the monastery. Whilst he was lingering there he died at Daphne, in We went to an estate which was six miles from Rossos and there, the Church of Saint Euphemia. As he was a stranger, the local two elders living in the world received us as their guests in the clergy buried him in the strangers’ burial-ground. The following day church on their property. Then one night we took some local men armed with weapons Domninos the patriarch <546-559>, He caused al Lhe city to come (on account of the wild animals) and climbed up the mountain to Daphne with candles and with the singing of psalms, to bring towards the light. We stayed there where the light was until dawn. forth the relics of that holy man. They buried him in the cemetery At day-break we noticed a little cave where the lights had appeared : where many relics of holy martyrs lie, and they built a small oratory and found the anchorite dead. He was wearing a hair-shirt and a over him, tunic of sack-cloth. He was holding a gospel-book <cnhanced with> i a silver cross. Beside him we found writing-tablets inscribed thus: 1, 1 89.
